zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset於1304年10月4日出生在英國薩默塞特斯托克 素布 哈姆頓時，他的父親Sir John Beauchamp30歲，母親Joan Chenduit25歲。他與Margaret Porter在英國英格蘭薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1343年5月19日在英國享年38歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset于1304年10月4日出生在英国薩默塞特斯托克 素布 哈姆頓时，他的父亲Sir John Beauchamp30岁，母亲Joan Chenduit25岁。他与Margaret Porter在英国英格兰薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1343年5月19日在英国享年38岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset was born on 4 October 1304, in Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England, his father, Sir John Beauchamp, was 30 and his mother, Joan Chenduit, was 25. He married Margaret Porter in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 19 May 1343, in England, at the age of 38, and was buried in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset est né le 4 octobre 1304, à Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Beauchamp, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Joan Chenduit, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Porter à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 19 mai 1343, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterré à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset am 4. Oktober 1304 in Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Beauchamp 30 und seine Mutter Joan Chenduit 25. Er hat in Somerset, England, United Kingdom Margaret Porter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 19. Mai 1343 im Alter von 38 Jahren in England und wurde in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Margaret Porter nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 19 maggio 1343, nel England, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolto a Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somersetが1304年10月4日にイギリスソメルセトストク スブ ハムドンで生まれた時，父Sir John Beauchampは30歳，母Joan Chenduitは25歳であった。イギリスイギリスソメルセトでMargaret Porterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1343年5月19日にイギリスで38歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトハトチ ビーウチャンップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset(이)가 1304년 10월 4일 영국 서머세트 스토크 수브 함던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Beauchamp(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Chenduit(은)는 25세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 Margaret Porter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1343년 5월 19일 영국에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에 묻혔다.

pt.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset nasceu em 4 de outubro de 1304, em Stoke sub Hamdon, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Beauchamp, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Joan Chenduit, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Porter em Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de maio de 1343, em Inglaterra, com 38 anos, e foi sepultado em Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ro.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset родился 4 октября 1304 г. в местности сток суб хамдон, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Beauchamp было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Chenduit, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Porter в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 19 мая 1343 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 38 лет и был похоронен в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Margaret Porter en Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 19 de mayo de 1343, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultado en Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
